reviews.review_title: Very frustrating to play. Not intuitive
reviews.review_description: I give two stars for the scare parts. The game is very difficult to play. Not very intuitive. The worst part is to keeping one of the lights on. I feel very sorry for people who are short. Also, it has the worst end of any game I have ever played. ￼
The game itself is great. Mentally tasking. Good horror. Genuinely scary. But the ending SUCKS!

reviews.review_title: WE need more content Amazing game
reviews.review_description:
This game IS Amazing but I would like to see a comfort setting, auto save and an épisode 2.
This game IS incredible and a little scary😱😭

Object interaction, and graphics (lighting) aren't anything to right home about, but the spooky ness factor is very well dialed in. I loved the shadow man coming out of the pictorial and then the shadow effect onthe wall and stuff. I wasn't smart enough to complete it, I got stuck at the fuses problem, but also it was really dark and I kinda give up when I start fumbling around without a light.

reviews.review_title: Amazing game but I found a bug
reviews.review_description: So I was playing through the game and freaking out. But then I opened the cabinet in the kitchen and... the door just came right off. Not a major bug but I just thought I should let someone know.

reviews.review_title: Fun and scary at the same time
reviews.review_description: This is so FUN I can NOT STOP PLAYING IT. I do not have anything else to say but ITS SO SO SO GOOD

I loved this game.  I don’t feel like the devs over  used jump scares and instead created a thriller reminiscent of original 7th Guest.  

The puzzles make sense in the environment which is key to feeling like you’re not just solving a puzzle.


The story line might seem a bit dark, but it has a good mystery to solve.

There are a few spoilers in my video play through but if you want to see some gameplay No Hope: The Escape on Oculus Quest 2
https://youtu.be/P_WA-4c8Cn0
It's crappie but idk how to delete it on my phone
